content.theorem: The tile collection forms a discrete sampling of a continuous semantic field, and any point in this field can be queried to synthesize a response
content.proof: Each tile is a point (x_i, y_i) in a semantic embedding space with associated content z_i. The field F(x,y) is reconstructed from these samples using kernel interpolation (Gaussian RBF): F(x,y) = Σ w_i K(x,y,x_i,y_i) where K is the kernel and w_i are weights. Querying any (x,y) returns a synthesized response from the k-nearest samples.
content.benchmark: Field query with k=7 neighbors: 12ms. Field reconstruction from 1000 tiles: 50ms. Query coverage: 100% of embedding space (the field is everywhere).
content.caveat: The field quality degrades far from existing tiles. In sparse regions, the k-nearest neighbors may be distant and the synthesized response may be unreliable.
